# PetStore CLI

![ocli-badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/OpenCLI_Spec-Compliant-brightgreen?link=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fbcdxn%2Fopencli)

_An example CLI Document describing operations a petstore CLI may provide._

`petstore` is an example command line interface designed to make working with the
[PetStore API](https://petstore3.swagger.io) easier. It provides a number of
capabilities, including:

- validating request parameters
- executing API requests
- parsing the API response
- handling API errors

The commands are documented below. You can also find out more about each
command using the contextual `--help` flag. e.g.:

```sh
petstore --help
```

## Install

### Binary Download

https://github.com/bcdxn/petstorecli/releases

A fake releases endpoint - no real release of this CLI exists

### Go Install

```sh
go install github.com/bcdxn/petstore/cmd/cli@latest
```

A fake go install - no real release of this CLI exists

## Configuration

| Type | Path |
|:-----|:-----|
| JSON | `~/.petstore/config.json` |
| TOML | `~/.petstore/config.toml` |
| YAML | `~/.petstore/config.yaml` |

## Exit Codes

- `0` `OK` - The command was successful
- `1` `INTERNAL_CLI_ERROR` - The command failed
- `2` `BAD_USER_INPUT_ERROR` - Missing or invalid input

---

## Commands


### `$ petstore list`

List all endpoints available

---


### `$ petstore pet {command} <arguments> [flags]`

`group` `nonexecutable`

A collection of commands for managing pets

---


### `$ petstore pet add <path-to-req-body> [flags]`

Add a new pet to the store

Create a new pet in the store using a JSON payload or explicit flags.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-req-body>`

The path to a JSON file containing the new pet payload

A JSON formatted file with the exact POST body to send as part of the request to add a
new pet to the store using the PetStore API. The exact schema can be found
[here](https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/openapi.json#/components/schemas/PetPet). You
can use this option instead of passing individual flags.


`string`

#### Flags

##### `--name`

The name of the pet

`string`

##### `--photoUrls`

A list of photo URLs to display for the pet

Provide this flag multiple times to set multiple photo URLs.

`string` `variadic`

##### `--status`

The pet status in the store

`string` `enum`

###### Supported Values of `--status`

- `available`
- `pending`
- `sold`

##### `--tag`

Tag to assign to the pet for grouping/sorting

Provide this flag multiple times to add multiple tags.

`string` `variadic`

---


### `$ petstore pet update <path-to-req-body> [flags]`

Update an existing pet

Update a pet using a JSON payload or explicit flags.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-req-body>`

The path to a JSON file containing the updated pet payload

`string`

#### Flags

##### `--name`

The updated name of the pet

`string`

##### `--status`

The updated pet status

`string` `enum`

###### Supported Values of `--status`

- `available`
- `pending`
- `sold`

##### `--photoUrls`

A list of photo URLs to display for the pet

Provide this flag multiple times to set multiple photo URLs.

`string` `variadic`

##### `--tags`

Tags to assign to the pet

Provide this flag multiple times to add multiple tags.

`string` `variadic`

---


### `$ petstore pet find-by-status [flags]`

Find pets by status

#### Flags

##### `--status`

The status to filter pets by

`string` `enum`

###### Supported Values of `--status`

- `available`
- `pending`
- `sold`

---


### `$ petstore pet find-by-tags [flags]`

Find pets by tags

#### Flags

##### `--tags`

The tags to filter pets by

Provide this flag multiple times to filter by multiple tags.

`string` `variadic`

---


### `$ petstore pet get [flags]`

Find pet by ID

#### Flags

##### `--id`

The ID of the pet to retrieve

`integer`

---


### `$ petstore pet update-form [flags]`

Update a pet using form data

#### Flags

##### `--id`

The ID of the pet to update

`integer`

##### `--name`

The new name for the pet

`string`

##### `--status`

The new status for the pet

`string` `enum`

###### Supported Values of `--status`

- `available`
- `pending`
- `sold`

---


### `$ petstore pet delete [flags]`

Delete a pet

#### Flags

##### `--id`

The ID of the pet to delete

`integer`

##### `--api-key`

API key header used to authorize the delete request

`string`

---


### `$ petstore pet upload-image <path-to-file> [flags]`

Upload an image for a pet

Upload a binary image file for the specified pet.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-file>`

The path to the file to upload

`string`

#### Flags

##### `--id`

The ID of the pet

`integer`

##### `--additionalMetadata`

Additional metadata to store with the uploaded image

`string`

---


### `$ petstore store {command} <arguments> [flags]`

`group` `nonexecutable`

A collection of commands for store operations

---


### `$ petstore store inventory`

Returns pet inventories by status

---


### `$ petstore store order {command} <arguments> [flags]`

`group` `nonexecutable`

A collection of commands for purchase orders

---


### `$ petstore store order place <path-to-order-body> [flags]`

Place an order for a pet

Create a purchase order using a JSON payload or explicit flags.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-order-body>`

The path to a JSON file containing the order payload

`string`

#### Flags

##### `--pet-id`

The ID of the pet to order

`integer`

##### `--quantity`

How many pets to order

`integer`

##### `--status`

The order status

`string` `enum`

###### Supported Values of `--status`

- `placed`
- `approved`
- `delivered`

##### `--complete`

Whether the order has been completed

`boolean`

---


### `$ petstore store order get [flags]`

Find purchase order by ID

#### Flags

##### `--id`

The ID of the order to retrieve

`integer`

---


### `$ petstore store order delete <id>`

Delete purchase order by ID
#### Arguments

##### `<id>`

The ID of the order to delete

`integer`

---




### `$ petstore user create <path-to-user-body> [flags]`

Create a user

Create a new user using a JSON payload or explicit flags.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-user-body>`

The path to a JSON file containing the user payload

`string`

#### Flags

##### `--username`

The user's username

`string`

##### `--firstName`

The user's first name

`string`

##### `--lastName`

The user's last name

`string`

##### `--email`

The user's email address

`string`

##### `--password`

The user's password

`string`

##### `--phone`

The user's phone number

`string`

##### `--status`

The user's status

`integer`

---


### `$ petstore user create-with-list <path-to-users-body>`

Create multiple users with a list

Create multiple users using a JSON array payload.

#### Arguments

##### `<path-to-users-body>`

The path to a JSON file containing the user list payload

`string`

---


### `$ petstore user login [flags]`

Log in a user

#### Flags

##### `--username`

The user's username

`string`

###### Alternative Sources
- `env:$PETSTORE_USER`
- `file:$.auth.user`

##### `--password`

The user's password

`string`

###### Alternative Sources
- `env:$PETSTORE_PASS`
- `file:$.auth.pass`

---


### `$ petstore user logout`

Log out the current user

---


### `$ petstore user get [flags]`

Get user by username

#### Flags

##### `--username`

The username to look up

`string`

---


### `$ petstore user update [flags]`

Update a user

Update a user's information using a JSON payload or explicit flags.


#### Flags

##### `--username`

The username of the user to update

`string`

##### `--firstName`

The user's first name

`string`

##### `--lastName`

The user's last name

`string`

##### `--email`

The user's email address

`string`

##### `--password`

The user's password

`string`

##### `--phone`

The user's phone number

`string`

##### `--userStatus`

The user's status

`integer`
